Fern PolrrrcAl Pnacrrcps CoMMrssroN
428 J Street r Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_2329
(916',322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
April28,2009
Mark Lilley
P.O. Box 572
Mount Hermon, CA 95041
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-09-095
Dear Mr. Lilley:
This letter re9.R9nds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Thii letteris based on the facts presented;
the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as the finder of fact
when it renders advice. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FppC Ops. 71.) Because your letter seeks
general information, we are providing you informal assisiance.2 
------- r -''
- 
Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
thereforeoffer no opinion on the application, if any, oi other conflict-of-interest Iaws such as
common law conflict of interest or Government code section 1090.
QUESTION
As an elected Director of the Felton Fire Frotection District, will you have reporting
obligations and a potential conflict of interest in decisions affect the Felton volunteer
Firefighters Association, if your son receives a college scholarship from the Association?
No, your son is not considered a member of your "immediate family,, for purposes of the
Act, therefore you will not have received as gift and you will not have a airquuiirying conflict of
interest in decisions affecting the Felton volinteer Firefighters Association.
I The Political Refbrm Act is contained in Government Code Secrions g 1000 through glol4. All sratutory
references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in sections l8l l0 through l8gg7 of ritle 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of thi California Code of'Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
I Intbrmal assistance does not provide the requester with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83t l4; Regulation ltt329(cXj).)
CONCLUSION


Our File No. I-09-095
Page Z
FACTS
You are an elected official currently serving on the Board of Directors of the Felton Fire
Protection District (the "District"). You are a former volunteer firefighter of the District and
during that time you were also a member of the Felton volunteer nirJrighters Association (the
"Associatiofl"), a non-profit organization that is a fund-raising club for virious causes and
activities. You stated that the Association is not technically aifiliated with the Fire District.
The Association funds a college scholarship for students from the local high school. The
scholarship funds are derived from private fundraiiing activities such as pancake breakfasts and
other events. Your son will be graduating from high Jchool this year urA pr*r on applying for
this scholarship. You indicated during a ielephone conversation Ln April io, zo1g,that your son
is currently 18 years old. You also stated thai the board of directors of the fire district has no
direct authority over the Felton Volunteer Fire Fighters Association and is not involved in the
scholarship decisions.
ANALYSIS
Under the Act, a public official has a conflict of interest if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of his/trer economic interests
unless the facts indicate that certain exceptions may apply. (Section g7100; Regulation 1g700.)
Step One and Two: Are you a public oflicial who will be making, participating in
making, or influencing a governmental decision? 
--o' E.
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to "public officials.,, (Sections
87-100, 87103; Regulation 18700(bxl).) A "public officia" is defined as ,.every member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency." (Section g204g.) you
are currently a member of the Board of Directors of th" Felton Fire Protection District serving as
an elected official, therefore qualifying as public official for purposes of the Act. A public
officia-l "makes a govemmental decision" when the official, icting within the authority of his or
her office or position, votes on a matter, appoints a person, obligales or commits his agency to
any course of action, or enters into any contractual igreement on behalf of his or her {"n"y.
(Regulation 18702.1.) As a member of the Board oiDirectors, you participate in decisions
relating to the District. Therefore, you will be participating in making u gou.**.ntal decision
as specified in the Act.
Step Three: Will the Felton Volunteer Firefighters Association be considered an
economic interest?
The relevant issue in your question is whether your son is considered a member of your
"immediate family" for purposes of being considered o, ""ono*ic interest under the Act. A
public official has a financial interest within the meaning of Section 87103 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financ[l effect, distinguishable from its effect


Our File No. I-09-095
page 3
on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or on any one
of five enumerated economic interests:
l. An economic interest in any business entity in which the public official has a direct or 
.
indirect invesrmenr worrh $2,000 or more. (Section g7103(a); Regulation lg703.l(a)); or in
any business entity in which the public official is a director, office-r, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d); Regulation lg703.l(b).)
2' An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect interest of
$2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b); Regulation lg7}3.Z.)
3' An economic interest in any source of income, including promised income, aggregating $500
or more within l2 months prior to the decision. (Sectionlszo:("); negutaii6a1rg7or.i.l
"Income" includes a pro rata share of any income of any business-entit! or trust in which the
individual or spouse owns, directly, indirectly or benefitialy, a lO-percent interest or greater.
(Section 82030(a).)
4. An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $420 or
more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Secrion 87103(e)] n"g,;i;ti;'" rc716.4.)
5' An economic interest in his or her personal finances, including those of his or her immediate
family. (Section 87103; Regulation 1g703.5.)
An official's "immediate family" includes the official's spouse and dependent children.
(Section 82029.) A child is considered "dependent" for purposes of the Act if the child is under
18 years old., and can be claimed as a dependent for fedeial irr.o-" tax purposes . (BogiiAdvice
Letter, No. 4-03-105.) You have indicated your son is 18 years old. For ptr.por". of the
::f:l 
:f-:ryr,estrProvisions of the Act your son will be ntt considered a member of ybur
tmmedtate family.' Therefore, if your son receives the scholarship from the Association, it will
not be considered a payment to you and therefore would not be ur."orro*ic interest for purposes
of the Act. 
'rv .rLr
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact this office at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
-(AR
I. Lemus
Intern, Legal Division
. 
I However, please note that a decision that impacts your son may still affect your personal flnances under
some circumstances' However, the facts you have prouia"a io not suggist that any such dfcision will occur. If you
are confronted with a decision in that may affect your personal finances, you should contact us for specific advice.
